title: Something at the door

stages.narration: A sound at the barricade — a slow, deliberate scrape of something dragging along the boards, right where you sleep. It comes again. Then a third time, patient, like it knows you're in here.

choices.label: Ease the shutter back and look

effects.note: the sound at the door was only the wind

choices.label: Don't move — wait it out until dawn

notes: It is the wind against a loose tarp. Nothing there. Checking costs a look and buys relief; waiting costs a sleepless night.
